sourceInspections.observation: Each live smoke constructs OpenAI::builder().build(), calls resolved_config(), and asserts resolved.base_url == DEFAULT_BASE_URL before making the live API request. The executed shell commands also explicitly unset OPENAI_BASE_URL.
The mocked contract asserts cancel uses /v1/evals/{eval_id}/runs/{run_id} by checking requests[4].path == /v1/evals/eval_123/runs/run_resp, i.e. no invented /cancel suffix.
The entitlement_skip_reason helpers only convert 403/404 API responses into explicit skip messages with status/code/message details; other errors still fail the test.

assertions.title: Fine-tuning job lifecycle is smoke-testable without paying for full training

steps.action: Run the ignored live fine-tuning job smoke with creds sourced, OPENAI_BASE_URL unset, and the assigned CARGO_TARGET_DIR.
steps.expected: The live smoke should prove create/retrieve/list/cancel/events on a tiny fine-tuning job and print request IDs.
steps.observed: Exit code 0; output printed request IDs for upload, processing, create, retrieve, list, cancel, and events, plus job id ftjob-lXx7wBAhGOjjpYJOg0in2pKC and cancel status cancelled.

steps.action: Inspect the live smoke source for base-url handling.
steps.expected: The smoke should assert default-host behavior when OPENAI_BASE_URL is unset.
steps.observed: tests/live_fine_tuning_job_smoke.rs asserts resolved_config().base_url == DEFAULT_BASE_URL before the live calls.

evidence.defaultHostProof: Source assertion plus OPENAI_BASE_URL unset in the executed command
evidence.network: Real API create/retrieve/list/cancel/events sequence completed successfully against the default host.

assertions.title: Containers CRUD preserves execution-policy fields without requiring tool execution

steps.action: Run the entitlement-aware live containers smoke.
steps.expected: Either an explicit entitlement skip reason should be printed or container create and related file operations should succeed with request IDs.
steps.observed: Exit code 0; no skip reason was printed. The output showed a real container id plus request IDs for create, container-file upload, retrieve, content, and delete.

steps.action: Inspect the containers live helper for skip handling.
steps.expected: 403/404 entitlement failures should become explicit skip messages instead of silent passes.
steps.observed: tests/containers_contract.rs maps only 403/404 API responses to a formatted skip reason; other failures still panic.

evidence.network: Real API container create plus container-file upload/retrieve/content/delete succeeded; no entitlement skip was required.
evidence.entitlementHandling: Source inspection confirms 403/404 would have been reported explicitly as skips.
